articles.title: OpenAI’s big Codex update is a direct shot at Claude Code
articles.summary: OpenAI upgraded Codex with new capabilities including computer control, image generation, and memory features to compete with Anthropic's Claude Code as AI rivalry intensifies.

articles.title: Google’s AI Mode update lets you open links without leaving the page
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode in Chrome now allows users to open source links in a side-by-side view instead of new tabs, enabling follow-up questions about the content without leaving the chat.

articles.title: Gemini can now pull from Google Photos to generate personalized images
articles.summary: Google's Gemini now uses Personal Intelligence to generate customized images by accessing Google Photos data, creating pictures that reflect your personal tastes and lifestyle based on connected apps.

articles.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
articles.summary: Factory, a three-year-old startup building AI coding tools for enterprises, raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures, reaching a $1.5 billion valuation.

articles.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
articles.summary: Luma launches a faith-focused production studio called Wonder Project. Its first project features Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley in a Moses story coming to Prime Video this spring.

articles.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
articles.summary: Upscale AI, an infrastructure company, seeks funding at $2B valuation just seven months after launch. The startup is in talks for its third funding round.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS with simpler, cost-effective solutions.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, prompting developers to switch to Goose, a free open-source alternative offering similar functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative $5K stunt attracted thousands of developers and successfully recruited engineers.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims it has attracted thousands of VMware customers dissatisfied with Broadcom's takeover strategy. The rival virtualization firm now has about 30,000 total customers, many migrating from VMware.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers at factories, water treatment centers, and refineries. Six US government agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers across 120 countries to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations targeting governments worldwide.

articles.title: Making AI operational in constrained public sector environments
articles.summary: Public sector organizations face pressure to adopt AI but encounter unique constraints around security and governance. Small language models offer a practical solution for operationalizing AI in government environments while addressing data security concerns.

articles.title: Treating enterprise AI as an operating layer
articles.summary: Enterprise AI's competitive advantage lies in owning the operating layer—software, data, feedback loops, and governance—rather than just accessing foundation models. This embedded approach compounds value over time, contrasting with treating AI as an on-demand utility.

articles.title: The Download: cyberscammers’ banking bypasses, and carbon removal troubles
articles.summary: Cyberscammers are bypassing bank security systems using illicit tools sold on Telegram to break facial recognition KYC checks. These exploits enable fraudulent account access and money laundering operations.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: Article discusses how AI systems can autonomously generate and submit pull requests, automating code improvements developers would typically make themselves.

articles.title: Training and Finetuning Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports training multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling developers to create custom models for better text and image understanding across diverse applications.

articles.title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
articles.summary: VAKRA explores AI agent reasoning, tool use capabilities, and failure modes. The research analyzes how agents interact with tools and identifies critical weaknesses in their decision-making processes.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and emerging trends in the field. The conversation explores what's coming next in artificial intelligence innovation.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity across operations. From Hot Wheels to content management, companies use AI to streamline workflows and enhance imaginative processes.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available. This initiative aims to democratize advanced farming solutions through open-source accessibility.

articles.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
articles.summary: Google Chrome introduces AI Mode upgrades that transform web interaction, offering new ways to explore and engage with online content through artificial intelligence capabilities.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 now generates personalized images using your Google Photos and personal context, creating visuals that reflect your unique life and experiences.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google has launched Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, an advanced text-to-speech technology offering expressive AI-generated speech. The tool is now available across Google's product ecosystem.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enhances autonomous robotics through improved spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding for real-world tasks.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate photorealistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI news including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: 5 top cloud migration software for Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
articles.summary: Cloud migration requires infrastructure as code platforms that support planning and execution. Infros exemplifies modern solutions by enabling teams to design and validate cloud architectures before deployment.

articles.title: OpenAI Agents SDK improves governance with sandbox execution
articles.summary: OpenAI introduces sandbox execution in its Agents SDK, enabling enterprise teams to deploy automated workflows with controlled risk and improved governance while maintaining access to frontier model capabilities.

articles.title: Cadence expands AI and robotic partnerships with Nvidia, Google Cloud
articles.summary: Cadence Design Systems expanded partnerships with Nvidia and Google Cloud to integrate AI with physics-based simulation for robotics and semiconductor design. The collaboration combines Cadence's simulation tools with Nvidia's CUDA-X and Omniverse for real-world system modeling.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging and system reliability.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance unveiled Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but weakens their ability to explore solutions on complex problems, revealing a critical tradeoff between efficiency and generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them emerge as the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to silently harvest credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, taking advantage of the OpenClaw AI agent boom.

articles.title: Qwen Team Open-Sources Qwen3.6-35B-A3B: A Sparse MoE Vision-Language Model with 3B Active Parameters and Agentic Coding Capabilities
articles.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team released Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, a sparse MoE vision-language model with 35B total parameters but only 3B active during inference, achieving competitive agentic coding performance with exceptional parameter efficiency.

articles.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Rosalind: Its First Life Sciences AI Model Built to Accelerate Drug Discovery and Genomics Research
articles.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-Rosalind, a specialized AI model designed to accelerate drug discovery and genomics research by automating time-consuming analytical work. The model is fine-tuned for biochemistry and genomics to support scientists rather than replace them.

articles.title: Building Transformer-Based NQS for Frustrated Spin Systems with NetKet
articles.summary: This tutorial combines Transformers with Neural Quantum States using NetKet and JAX to solve frustrated spin systems, leveraging global attention mechanisms for capturing complex quantum correlations in high-dimensional physics problems.

articles.title: I traded my Sonos Era 300 for Denon's new home speaker - and see no reason to go back
articles.summary: The Denon Home 400 speaker offers a compelling alternative to Sonos Era 300, featuring upgraded hardware and software with a modern design that justifies switching.

articles.title: AI-powered website builders have come a long way - here's your best option in 2026
articles.summary: AI-powered website builders have significantly improved from last year and can now effectively handle website creation tasks. The article identifies the best AI website builder options available in 2026.

articles.title: Amazon just slashed $250 off the Google Pixel 10 - and a Prime subscription isn't required
articles.summary: Amazon reduced the Google Pixel 10 price by $250 to $549, delivering flagship performance and excellent camera capabilities without requiring a Prime membership.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article explores using Python decorators in production machine learning engineering. Decorators help streamline ML workflows and improve code organization for scalable systems.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article discusses five techniques for optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems when processing long contexts, improving efficiency and performance in AI applications.

articles.title: How to Implement Tool Calling with Gemma 4 and Python
articles.summary: Guide on implementing tool calling with Gemma 4 using Python. Demonstrates how to leverage the open-weights model for function calling capabilities.

articles.title: What It Actually Takes to Run Code on 200M€ Supercomputer
articles.summary: MareNostrum V is a 200M€ supercomputer using SLURM schedulers and fat-tree topologies to manage 8,000 nodes. Running code requires understanding complex scheduling and network infrastructure.

articles.title: Your Chunks Failed Your RAG in Production
articles.summary: Chunking strategy is critical for RAG systems in production, as poor chunking decisions cannot be fixed by models or LLMs afterward. Proper data preparation upstream is essential for RAG success.

articles.title: Building My Own Personal AI Assistant: A Chronicle, Part 2
articles.summary: Author describes building a task breaker module for their personal AI assistant that decomposes complex goals into actionable steps, significantly improving AI-driven productivity workflows.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's sci-fi action game PRAGMATA launches on GeForce NOW simultaneously with its worldwide release, allowing cloud streaming to any device. Five additional titles join the platform this week.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have evolved into AI token factories, making cost per token the primary metric for assessing AI infrastructure economics rather than traditional compute costs or FLOPS per dollar.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe Premiere introduces a new Color Grading Mode accelerated by NVIDIA RTX GPUs, announced at NAB Show 2026. The beta feature aims to enhance creative workflows for video editors and professional content creators.

articles.title: Cost-efficient custom text-to-SQL using Amazon Nova Micro and Amazon Bedrock on-demand inference
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Micro with Bedrock on-demand inference enables cost-efficient custom text-to-SQL generation using LoRA fine-tuning, eliminating continuous infrastructure costs while maintaining production-grade accuracy for specialized database dialects.

articles.title: Transform retail with AWS generative AI services
articles.summary: AWS enables retailers to reduce returns and boost confidence using generative AI virtual try-on technology built with Amazon Nova Canvas and Rekognition, improving profitability and customer satisfaction.

articles.title: How Automated Reasoning checks in Amazon Bedrock transform generative AI compliance
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock's Automated Reasoning checks replace probabilistic AI validation with mathematical verification, enabling regulated industries to produce provably correct, auditable AI outputs.

articles.title: How to Build Vision AI Pipelines Using NVIDIA DeepStream Coding Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA DeepStream coding agents simplify building real-time vision AI pipelines, reducing development complexity and code requirements for developers creating advanced computer vision applications.

articles.title: Building Custom Atomistic Simulation Workflows for Chemistry and Materials Science with NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit
articles.summary: NVIDIA's ALCHEMI toolkit enables custom atomistic simulation workflows for chemistry and materials science, balancing computational accuracy and speed. It addresses the trade-off between expensive ab initio methods and faster classical approaches.

articles.title: NVIDIA NVbandwidth: Your Essential Tool for Measuring GPU Interconnect and Memory Performance
articles.summary: NVIDIA NVbandwidth is an essential tool for measuring GPU interconnect and memory performance in CUDA applications. It helps developers optimize data transfer performance in single and multi-GPU systems.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, exploring generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce their environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough innovations in XR development.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. The work focuses on feature attribution to understand decision-making processes for safer, more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: This article presents information-driven design methods for imaging systems that optimize how optical systems encode objects into images while managing noise corruption. The approach uses information estimation from noisy measurements to improve imaging performance.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability challenges and enabling better performance on long-horizon tasks.
